title: Google limits Meta’s use of its Gemini AI models: Report
snippet: Google has limited Meta's access to its Gemini AI models due to high demand. This has disrupted Meta's internal AI projects and affected other Google clients. Despite growing Google Cloud revenue, computing power constraints hindered further growth in the sector.

title: Fake GTA 6 early access websites are stealing gamers' money: Here's how the scam works
snippet: Cybersecurity researchers warn that scammers are exploiting GTA 6 hype by offering fake early access for cryptocurrency payments.&nbsp;

title: Soaring iPad, Xbox prices reveal pain of memory chip mess
snippet: Apple and Microsoft increased prices for Xbox consoles, Macs, and iPads due to a memory chip shortage driven by AI demand.

title: Moto Pad 70 Pro launched in India with 13-inch 3.5K display, AI features and 10,200mAh battery
snippet: The Moto Pad 70 Pro features a 13-inch 3.5K display, Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, and quad speaker setup. It supports 2TB expandable storage and runs on Android 16

title: OpenAI names Prabhjeet Singh India MD, launches GPT-5.6 Sol with stronger cyber protections
snippet: OpenAI has appointed Prabhjeet Singh as Managing Director for India. The company has also announced a limited release of its GPT-5.6 model, which features three variants: Sol, Terra, and Tuna

title: iPhone 18 to get a big RAM upgrade as Apple doubles down on AI: Report
snippet: Apple is set to launch the iPhone 18 and iPhone 18e next spring with 9GB and the A20 chipset. The iPhone 18 lineup could also include improved AI features and brighter display, and better optics.

title: India's robotics funding doubles in 2026 first-half, but scale seems far away
snippet: India's robotics sector continues to lag its global peers. In 2025, Indian robotics startups raised less than 1% of the capital secured by US robotics startups and about 2% of that raised in China, according to Tracxn.

title: iPhone Ultra launch back on track after Apple fixes critical foldable issue: Report
snippet: Apple's foldable iPhone is set for a September launch after resolving hinge issues, as per a new report

title: Apple price hike: Check updated prices of MacBook Neo, Mac mini M4, iPads and HomePod
snippet: Apple has raised prices for its MacBooks, iPads, and other products in India due to increasing memory chip costs.&nbsp;

title: Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices amid memory chip crunch — Check new prices here
snippet: Memory chip makers such as Micron have, in recent months, prioritised orders from AI chipmakers like Nvidia, helping them earn record profits but leaving little supply for electronics makers that have been forced to increase prices.

snippet: Schools and other organizations are posting children's photos and achievements online without proper parental consent, potentially violating the DPDP Act. snippet: Australia is doubling penalties for tech firms failing to enforce its child social media ban, as evidence shows limited impact on teen usage. title: Beyond Nvidia: Why AI giants like OpenAI, Google and Amazon are building custom chips
snippet: The AI race is heating up as tech giants like OpenAI, Google, Amazon, and Microsoft design their own chips to optimize AI operations. title: Inside Claude Mythos: Why Anthropic held back its most advanced AI
snippet: Anthropic's advanced AI, Claude Mythos, capable of discovering software flaws, was kept from public release due to misuse concerns. title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 AI models with phased rollout and stronger safety checks
snippet: OpenAI has unveiled GPT-5.6, its latest AI model family with Sol, Terra and Luna. The models will roll out gradually across ChatGPT, the API and Codex, with Sol adding Max and Ultra modes for advanced reasoning.

title: 'Humans behave better when watched': Xprice founder, tech leaders make the case for surveillance
snippet: Tech leaders like Peter Diamandis and Larry Ellison are championing a future of 'radical transparency' enabled by a vast network of sensors. They argue that constant monitoring, from home cameras to orbiting satellites, will foster better human behaviour as 'no one can hide.'

title: Companies pull back on AI as weak workplace collaboration hurts results: Report
snippet: A recent report shows that many companies are pivoting away from AI initiatives due to challenges with quality and employee engagement. title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.
snippet: It’s been hot in London this week. Really hot. A dangerous heat wave has hit Western Europe. Yesterday, the UK recorded its highest ever June temperature at 36.1 °C (about 97 °F). snippet: It’s been hard to look away from headlines about the European heat wave this week. title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
snippet: IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology announced in 2021. title: Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants
snippet: Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid is being pushed to its limits as people turn to fans and air-conditioning to try to stay cool.
